constructs.description: Creates a new instance of a class. The class name goes in braces; constructor arguments use §A. Use §NEW{ClassName} §/NEW for zero-argument constructors.
Declares a variable binding. Use ~ prefix for mutable variables. Immutable by default.

examples.csharp: int mutableCount = 0; // needs to be modified later

constructs.description: Defines a method within a class. Supports visibility (pub/priv/prot) and modifiers such as virt/vr, over/ov, abs, stat/st, and seal.
Defines a standalone function (not a class member). Use for top-level functions or static utility methods.

examples.csharp: public static int Add(int a, int b) { return a + b; }

constructs.description: Creates an anonymous function/lambda expression. The id is required. Lambda parameters are name:type pairs after the id. Can capture variables from enclosing scope.
Defines a property with optional getter and setter. Auto-properties use accessor shorthand such as get,set, get,priset, or get,init. Full properties use indented §GET and §SET bodies.

examples.csharp: public int Count { get; private set; }
public int Value { get { return _value; } set { _value = value; } }

constructs.description: Defines an indexer (this[]) on a class or interface. Compact auto-property form puts accessors and parameters inline. Full form uses indented §GET/§SET/§INIT accessor blocks.

examples.csharp: public int this[int index] { get; set; }
public string this[string key] { get; set; }
public int this[int row, int col] { get; set; }
public int this[int i] { get { return _data[i]; } set { _data[i] = value; } }

constructs.calorSyntax: Property: use bare member expression (e.g., obj.Property). Method: use §C{obj.Method} args.
constructs.description: §C{...} emits parentheses, making it a method call. For property or field access (no parentheses), use a bare dotted expression in a binding (§B{var} obj.Property) or directly as an expression. Do NOT use §C for property access — it will emit obj.Property() with unwanted parens.
For loop with an inclusive integer range. The body is indented under the loop header.
Iterates over each element in a collection.
Executes body while condition is true.
Conditional branching. §EI for else-if, §EL for else. Both are optional.
Conditional expression (ternary operator). Uses prefix notation with (? ...) syntax. Both branches are expressions, not statements.
Returns a value from a function or method. Use bare §R for void returns.
Defines a class. Supports modifiers such as abs, seal, partial, stat, struct, and readonly. Use §EXT for base class and §IMPL for interfaces.
Defines an async function. Use §AF for standalone async functions, §AMT for async class methods.

examples.csharp: public async Task ProcessAsync() { await DoWork(); }

constructs.description: Awaits an asynchronous operation.
Exception handling. §TR for try block, §CA for catch (with optional type and variable), §FI for finally.
Throws an exception. Use §RT to rethrow in a catch block.
Declares side effects a function may have. Common effects: cw (console write), db (database), net (network), fs (file system), rnd (random), time, and env.

examples.csharp: // Method that reads file and writes console

constructs.description: Declares a precondition that must be true when the function is called. Part of Design by Contract.

examples.calor:
§F{f001:Process:pub} (str:input) -> void
  §Q{"input cannot be null"} (!= input null)
  ...

constructs.description: Declares a postcondition that will be true when the function returns. Use result to refer to the return value.

examples.calor:
§F{f002:GetItems:pub} () -> List<Item>
  §S{"must return at least one item"} (> result.Count 0)
  ...

constructs.description: Declares a class invariant that must always be true for valid instances.
Calls a method or function. Use §C{target} followed by arguments; §A and §/C may be used to close inline nested calls where needed.
Defines a field in a class. Usually private, prefixed with underscore by convention.
Defines an interface with method signatures.

examples.csharp: public interface IRepository { void Save(Entity e); }

constructs.description: Defines an enumeration. Enum values are indented under §EN and may use = for explicit values.

examples.csharp: public enum Status { Active, Inactive, Pending }
public enum Priority : byte { Low = 0, High = 1 }

constructs.description: Defines a class constructor. Use §BASE with §A arguments to call a base constructor, or §THIS to chain constructors.

constructs.calorSyntax: §W{id:expr} expression with indented §K pattern → result cases
constructs.description: Pattern matching switch expression. §K introduces each case/pattern. Use _ for default.

examples.csharp: shape switch { Circle c => c.Area(), Rectangle r => r.Width * r.Height }

constructs.calorSyntax: (?? left right) for null-coalescing, (?. target Member) for null-conditional
constructs.description: Handles nullable values. (?? left right) returns left if non-null, else right. (?. target Member) accesses Member on target only if non-null. Both use Lisp-style prefix notation inside parentheses.
Resource management with automatic disposal.

constructs.calorSyntax: Use type parameters in names, e.g. §CL{c001:Repository<T>:pub}; constraints use §WHERE T : class
constructs.description: Generic type parameters and constraints.

examples.csharp: public class Repository<T> where T : class { }
public T Get<T>(int id) where T : new() { }

constructs.calorSyntax: Use method syntax with lambdas: §C{collection.Where} lambdaValue
constructs.description: LINQ queries use method-chain syntax with §C (call) and §LAM (lambda) in Calor. Complex chains are commonly hoisted into bindings before the final call.

examples.csharp: from item in items where item.Active select item.Name

constructs.description: String interpolation uses ${} inside ordinary string literals. Note: ${0}, ${1} etc. are treated as format placeholders, not interpolation.
Loop control statements.
Defines an immutable data record.

examples.csharp: public record Person(string Name, int Age);

constructs.description: Creates and initializes a fixed-size array. The element type is specified in the header. Elements are space-separated between the opening and closing tags. Supports both explicit (new int[] { ... }) and bare ({ ... }) initializer syntax.
Defines a static class (utility class). Use 'stat' modifier.
Defines a virtual method that can be overridden in derived classes. Use the 'virt' modifier (the compiler also accepts 'vr').
Overrides a virtual method from a base class. Use the 'over' modifier (the compiler also accepts 'ov').
Defines an abstract class that cannot be instantiated directly. Use 'abs' modifier.
Defines an abstract method with no body that must be overridden. Use 'abs' modifier.
Defines a sealed class that cannot be inherited from. Use 'seal' modifier.
Acquires an exclusive lock on an object for thread-safe access.
Marks todo items, fixmes, or hacks in the code.

examples.csharp: // FIXME: This breaks with null input
// HACK: Temporary workaround for API bug

constructs.description: Marks code as deprecated with a reason.
Documents the algorithmic complexity of a function.
Visibility modifiers are specified in the tag header. Use 'pub' for public, 'priv' for private, 'prot' for protected.
Defines an event. Simple form has no body. For custom add/remove accessors, use indented §EADD and §EREM blocks inside the event body.

examples.csharp: public event EventHandler Click { add { _click += value; } remove { _click -= value; } }

constructs.description: Defines an operator overload for a class or struct. Supports binary operators (+, -, *, /, ==, etc.), unary operators (-, !, ~, ++, --), and conversion operators (implicit, explicit).

examples.csharp: public static MyType operator+(MyType left, MyType right) { return new MyType(left.Value + right.Value); }
public static MyType operator-(MyType value) { return new MyType(-value.Value); }
public static implicit operator int(MyType value) { return value.Value; }
public static explicit operator MyType(int value) { return new MyType(value); }

constructs.calorSyntax: DECIMAL:value or DEC:value or value with m/M suffix
constructs.description: Decimal literals for precise numeric values. Use DECIMAL: or DEC: prefix, or append m/M suffix.
Single Unicode character. Uses 'char' type name directly (no abbreviation).
Produces values lazily from an iterator method. §YIELD returns a value, §YBRK stops iteration.

examples.csharp: public IEnumerable<int> GetNumbers() { for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) yield return i; }

constructs.calorSyntax: §DEL{id:name} with indented §I parameters and optional §O return type
constructs.description: Defines a delegate type (function signature). Use for callbacks, event handlers, and function references. §DEL can appear at the top level or nested inside §CL class bodies.

examples.csharp: public class MyClass { public delegate void Handler(int x); }

constructs.description: Conditional compilation blocks. §PP for #if, §PPE for #else/#elif, §/PP to close. Code inside is conditionally included based on defined symbols.
Defines a value type (struct). Uses the 'struct' modifier on §CL. Supports readonly structs with the 'readonly' modifier.

examples.csharp: public struct Point { public int X; public int Y; }
public readonly struct Vector2D { public readonly double X; public readonly double Y; }

constructs.description: Constrains generic type parameters. Supports class, struct, new(), interface, and base class constraints.

examples.csharp: public class Repository<T> where T : class, IEntity { }
public T Create<T>() where T : new() { return new T(); }
public void Process<T>(T item) where T : struct { }

constructs.description: Executes the body at least once, then continues while condition is true. The body is indented under the §DO header.
Defines part of a class that can be split across multiple files. Uses the 'partial' modifier.

examples.csharp: public partial class UserService { public void Save() { } }

constructs.description: Marks a block for unsafe pointer operations. Required for pointer arithmetic, fixed statements, and stackalloc.

constructs.calorSyntax: §IF{id} (== x null) with indented §ASSIGN x default
constructs.description: C#'s ??= operator has no direct Calor equivalent. Use an §IF null check with §ASSIGN as a workaround.
Attaches metadata to declarations. Placed before the declaration they annotate.
C# 'is' patterns with and/or/not combinators are decomposed into standard boolean expressions using prefix notation. No special tag needed — fully supported natively.
C# range expressions (start..end) and index-from-end (^n) are fully supported. Use §RANGE for slicing and §^ for indexing from the end.

constructs.calorSyntax: Supported in C# → Calor migration. Decompose to §IF chains for hand-written Calor.
constructs.description: Positional patterns (tuple deconstruction) and property patterns are fully supported during C# → Calor migration — the converter preserves them inline. For hand-written Calor, decompose complex patterns into §IF/§EI chains with explicit comparisons.

#pragma warning directives are NOT supported in Calor. They are silently dropped during C# → Calor conversion. If you need to suppress specific warnings, use §CSHARP{#pragma warning disable XXXX}§/CSHARP as a raw passthrough.

constructs.calorSyntax: (Type1, Type2) for types; (expr1, expr2) for literals
constructs.description: Tuple types use parenthesized comma-separated types: (i32, str). Tuple literals use parenthesized comma-separated expressions: (1, "hello"). Named tuple elements are supported: (i32 X, i32 Y). Tuples are distinguished from Lisp expressions by the presence of commas.
Null-coalescing operator in Lisp-style prefix notation. Returns left if non-null, otherwise returns right. Use inside parentheses: (?? expr default).
Null-conditional member access in Lisp-style prefix notation. Accesses MemberName on target only if target is non-null, otherwise returns null. The member name is an unquoted identifier.
Jump to a specific case label or to the default case in a switch statement. For regular goto, use §GOTO{labelName}.

§NEW.name: New Instance
§NEW.description: Creates a new instance of a class using its constructor.

§B.name: Binding
§B.description: Variable binding. Immutable by default, use ~ prefix for mutable variables.
§B.csharpEquivalent: var name = value; or int name = value; (mutable)

§V.name: Binding (Alias)
§V.description: Alternative syntax for variable binding. Identical to §B{name} value.

§F.name: Function
§F.description: Defines a standalone function. Not a class member.

§AF.name: Async Function
§AF.csharpEquivalent: public static async Task<T> Name(params) { body }

§M.name: Function (Alias)
§M.description: Alternative syntax for function definition. Identical to §F.

§MT.name: Method
§MT.description: Defines a class method. Modifiers include virt/vr, over/ov, abs, stat/st, and seal.

§I.name: Input Parameter
§I.syntax: (type:name) or (type:name:modifier) or (type:name = defaultExpr) in a header signature
§I.description: Header parameter syntax. Modifiers: 'this' (extension method), 'ref', 'out', 'in', 'params'. Default values use =expr in the signature.
§I.csharpEquivalent: Type name, this Type name, ref Type name, params Type[] name, Type name = default

examples.name: Extension Method Parameter
examples.description: Marks this as an extension method parameter (C# 'this' modifier)

§O.name: Output Type
§O.description: Declares the return type of a function or method via the header arrow.

§R.name: Return
§R.description: Returns a value from a function. Empty for void returns.

§CL.name: Class
§CL.description: Defines a class. Modifiers include abs, seal, partial, stat, struct, and readonly.

§EXT.name: Extends
§EXT.description: Specifies the base class for inheritance.

§IMPL.name: Implements
§IMPL.description: Specifies an interface the class implements.

§L.name: Loop
§L.description: For loop over an inclusive integer range. Use §EACH for foreach loops.
§L.csharpEquivalent: for (var i = start; i < end; i++) or foreach (var item in collection)

§WH.name: While Loop
§WH.description: While loop that continues while condition is true.

§DO.name: Do-While Loop
§DO.description: Do-while loop that executes at least once.

§TR.name: Try Block
§TR.description: Exception handling with try, catch, and optional finally.
§TR.csharpEquivalent: try { body } catch (Type var) { handler } finally { cleanup }

§CA.name: Catch Block
§CA.description: Catches exceptions. Type and variable name are optional.

§TH.name: Throw
§TH.description: Throws an exception. Use §RT to rethrow the current exception.

§GOTO.name: Goto
§GOTO.description: Jump to a label, a switch case label, or the default case. Use CASE: prefix for goto case and DEFAULT for goto default.
§GOTO.csharpEquivalent: goto label; or goto case expr; or goto default;

§E.name: Effects
§E.description: Declares side effects. Common: cw (console write), cr (console read), fs:r/fs:w (file), net (network), db (database).
§E.csharpEquivalent: No direct equivalent - annotations or documentation

§Q.name: Precondition
§Q.description: Declares a precondition that must be true when function is called.

§S.name: Postcondition
§S.description: Declares a postcondition that will be true when function returns. Use result for return value.

§PRE.name: Precondition (Alias)
§PRE.description: Alternative syntax for precondition. Identical to §Q.

§POST.name: Postcondition (Alias)
§POST.description: Alternative syntax for postcondition. Identical to §S.

§INV.name: Invariant
§INV.description: Class invariant that must always be true.

§PROP.name: Property
§PROP.description: Defines a property. Positional args: id, name, type, visibility, optional modifiers/accessors. Example: §PROP{p001:Count:i32:pub:stat:get,set} for a public static int property.

§SET.name: Setter
§SET.description: Property setter with optional visibility modifier.
§SET.csharpEquivalent: set { body } or private set { body }

§FLD.name: Field
§FLD.description: Defines a class field. Modifiers: ro (readonly), st (static).

§THIS.name: This Constructor
§THIS.description: Chains to another constructor in the same class.

§EVT.name: Event
§EVT.description: Defines an event. Positional attributes: id, name, visibility, delegateType. Simple form for field-like events; use indented §EADD/§EREM blocks for custom add/remove logic.
§EVT.csharpEquivalent: public event DelegateType Name; or event with add/remove accessors

§EADD.name: Event Add Accessor
§EADD.description: Custom add accessor body inside an event definition (§EVT). Contains statements executed when a handler is subscribed.

§EREM.name: Event Remove Accessor
§EREM.description: Custom remove accessor body inside an event definition (§EVT). Contains statements executed when a handler is unsubscribed.

§LAM.name: Lambda
§LAM.description: Anonymous function/lambda expression. Inline arrow form preferred for simple expressions.

§W.name: Switch/Match
§W.syntax: §W{id:expr} expression with indented §K pattern → result cases

§K.name: Case
§K.description: A case in a switch expression. Use _ for default.

§WHERE.name: Generic Constraint
§WHERE.description: Constraint on a generic type parameter.

§?.name: Null-Conditional (Legacy)
§?.description: Legacy null-conditional syntax. Prefer Lisp-style (?. target Member) for null-conditional access.

§??.name: Null-Coalescing (Legacy)
§??.description: Legacy null-coalescing syntax. Prefer Lisp-style (?? left right) for null-coalescing.

§?..name: Null-Conditional Access
§?..description: Null-conditional member access in Lisp prefix notation. Returns null if target is null, otherwise accesses Member.

§STR.name: String Interpolation (NOT IMPLEMENTED)
§STR.syntax: NOT VALID — use inline ${} or §INTERP instead
§STR.description: §STR is not a valid section marker. For string interpolation, use inline ${} within ordinary string literals, e.g., "Hello ${name}".

§INTERP.name: String Interpolation
§INTERP.description: Builds an interpolated string from literal parts and §EXP expression parts. Strings are opaque — expressions cannot be embedded inside quotes. Use separate "literal" and §EXP expr parts.

§RAW.name: Raw C# Passthrough
§RAW.description: Embeds raw C# code that is emitted verbatim without any transformation. Use this escape hatch for C# constructs that Calor does not yet support (e.g., LINQ query syntax, complex expression trees). Content between §RAW and §/RAW is not tokenized or parsed as Calor.

§M.name: Module
§M.description: Defines a module (namespace/compilation unit). All code must be inside a module.

§C.name: Call
§C.description: Calls a function or method. Target can be a function name, a method chain (e.g., obj.Method), or a nested expression. Arguments are marked with §A. Always emits parentheses — for property access, use bare member expressions instead.

§U.name: Using Import
§U.description: Imports a namespace or creates an alias. Must appear inside a module (§M{id:Name}) block.
§U.csharpEquivalent: using Namespace; or using Alias = Namespace;

§SM.name: Some (Option)
§SM.description: Creates a Some value for Option types. Represents a present value.

§NN.name: None (Option)
§NN.description: Creates a None value for Option types. Represents absence of value.

§OK.name: Ok (Result)
§OK.description: Creates an Ok result for Result types. Represents success.

§ERR.name: Err (Result)
§ERR.description: Creates an Err result for Result types. Represents failure.

§LIST.name: List
§LIST.csharpEquivalent: new List<Type>() or new List<Type> { elements }

§P.name: Print
§P.description: Prints to console with newline. Use §Pf for no newline.

§EACH.name: Foreach
§EACH.description: Iterates over each element in a collection. Type is inferred automatically. Use three-field form §EACH{id:var:type} for explicit typing.

§IV.name: Invariant
§IV.description: Class invariant that must always be true. Alias for §INV.

§SIG.name: Signature
§SIG.description: Declares a method signature in an interface.

§RT.name: Rethrow
§RT.description: Rethrows the current exception in a catch block.

§VAR.name: Variable Pattern
§VAR.description: Captures a value into a variable in pattern matching.

§WHEN.name: Guard Clause
§WHEN.description: Adds a guard condition to a pattern match case.

§A.name: Argument
§A.description: Marks an argument in inline calls or constructors.

§VR.name: Virtual Modifier
§VR.description: Marks a method as virtual, allowing it to be overridden in derived classes.

§OV.name: Override Modifier
§OV.description: Marks a method as overriding a virtual method from a base class.

§AB.name: Abstract Modifier
§AB.description: Marks a method or class as abstract. Abstract methods have no body.
§AB.csharpEquivalent: public abstract ReturnType Name(); or public abstract class Name

§SD.name: Sealed Modifier
§SD.description: Marks a class as sealed, preventing inheritance.

§SW.name: Switch/Match
§SW.description: Pattern matching switch expression. Prefer §W with indented §K cases.

§BODY.name: Body Start
§BODY.syntax: Indent the body under the header
§BODY.description: Explicitly marks the start of a function or method body. Optional in most cases.

§TD.name: Todo
§TD.description: Marks a todo item in the code.

§FX.name: Fixme
§FX.description: Marks a fixme item in the code indicating something that needs to be fixed.

§HK.name: Hack
§HK.description: Marks a hack or workaround in the code.

§EX.name: Example
§EX.description: Inline example or test code in documentation.

§SN.name: Since
§SN.description: Documents the version when a feature was introduced.

§DP.name: Deprecated
§DP.description: Marks a feature as deprecated with a reason.

§BR.name: Breaking Change
§BR.description: Documents a breaking change in the API.

§XP.name: Experimental
§XP.description: Marks a feature as experimental and subject to change.

§SB.name: Stable
§SB.description: Marks a feature as stable and production-ready.

§DC.name: Decision
§DC.description: Documents an architectural or design decision with alternatives considered.

§CHOSEN.name: Chosen Alternative
§CHOSEN.description: Documents the chosen alternative in a decision.

§CT.name: Context
§CT.description: Documents the context or background for a decision or feature.

§VS.name: Visible Section
§VS.description: Marks a section as visible (not hidden).

§HD.name: Hidden Section
§HD.description: Marks a section as hidden from normal view.

§FC.name: Focus
§FC.description: Marks a section as the current focus area for editing.

§CX.name: Complexity
§CX.description: Documents the algorithmic complexity of a function or method.

§AS.name: Assume
§AS.description: Documents an assumption that must be true at this point in the code.

§UB.name: Used By
§UB.description: Documents what uses this code (reverse dependency).

§LK.name: Lock
§LK.description: Acquires a lock on an object for thread-safe access.

§SYNC.name: Synchronization Block
§SYNC.description: Lock/synchronization block. Preferred over §LK for converter output. Compiles to C# lock statement.

§TASK.name: Task Reference
§TASK.description: References a task or issue ID.

§DATE.name: Date Marker
§DATE.description: Marks a date associated with the code (e.g., when added or modified).

§AU.name: Author
§AU.description: Documents the author of the code.

§US.name: Uses
§US.description: Declares dependencies or imports used by the code.

§FL.name: Field (Alias)
§FL.description: Alias for §FLD. Defines a field in a class.

§WR.name: Where Constraint (Legacy)
§WR.description: Legacy syntax for generic type constraints. Prefer §WHERE.

§ENUM.name: Enum (Legacy)
§ENUM.description: Legacy syntax for enum definition. Prefer §EN.

§EEXT.name: Enum Extension
§EEXT.description: Defines extension methods for an enum type.

§END_BODY.name: End Body
§END_BODY.description: Explicitly marks the end of a function or method body. Pairs with §BODY. Optional in most cases.

§RANGE.name: Range
§RANGE.description: Represents a range value (start..end) for array/span slicing.

§^.name: Index From End
§^.description: Indexes from the end of a collection. Equivalent to C#'s ^n operator.
